verbGlossary.APPROVED FOR: {source} approval authorizes {target} to proceed (e.g., temp CO approves partial occupancy during facility transition).
verbGlossary.BLOCKS: Unresolved permit or regulatory item prevents {target} from completing (e.g., open permits block contract closeout).
verbGlossary.CLOSES OUT: {source} action formally concludes {target} (e.g., certificate of completion closes out contract closeout).
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINS: {source} limits or shapes what can occur during {target} (e.g., zoning constrains design, environmental permit constrains demolition scope).
verbGlossary.COORDINATED WITH: Inspection scheduling must align with {target} timeline to avoid delays (e.g., inspection scheduling coordinated with foundation pours).
verbGlossary.DELAYS: {source} hold actively pauses or slows progress on {target} until the hold is resolved.
verbGlossary.ENABLES: {source} or approval unlocks {target} to begin or proceed (e.g., building permit enables earthwork).
verbGlossary.FOLLOWS: {source} permit activity occurs sequentially after {target} completes (e.g., permit application follows estimating).
verbGlossary.GATES: {source} review is a hard prerequisite that must pass before {target} can be entered (e.g., plan review gates permitting).
verbGlossary.GOVERNS: {source} has ongoing jurisdiction over work performed during {target} (e.g., fire marshal governs fire protection rough-in).
verbGlossary.INITIATED DURING: {source} permit process begins within the timeframe of {target} (e.g., permit application initiated during permitting).
verbGlossary.INSPECTED DURING: {source} inspector examines work in progress during {target}; must pass before concealment (e.g., AHJ inspects framing before drywall).
verbGlossary.ISSUED AFTER: {source} or permit is formally issued upon satisfactory completion of {target} (e.g., CO issued after final inspections).
verbGlossary.ISSUED DURING: {source} is formally granted within the timeframe of {target} (e.g., building permit issued during permitting).
verbGlossary.MEASURES: {source}quantifies the performance or duration of {target} (e.g., permit cycle time measures permitting, inspection pass rate measures final inspections).
verbGlossary.REQUIRED BEFORE: {source} or regulatory action must be obtained before {target} can legally begin (e.g., demolition permit required before demolition).
verbGlossary.REVIEWS: {source} evaluates plans or documents produced during {target} (e.g., building department reviews design development).
verbGlossary.TRACKED DURING: {source} status or metric is monitored throughout {target} (e.g., permit status tracked during permitting).
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source} filing initiates a required activity within {target} (e.g., OSHA notification triggers safety orientation).
verbGlossary.VALIDATES: {source}testing or inspection confirms {target} work meets code and performance standards (e.g., third-party testing validates TAB).
verbGlossary.VERIFIED DURING: {source}documentation or labor requirements are checked against records during {target} (e.g., compliance docs verified during as-built documentation).
